task_1_answer: Shots and goals happen mostly after few passes, especially 0-3 passes.
task_2_answer: The 4-6 pass category has the highest conversion rate, but this does not prove it is always best.
task_3_answer: Brazil, France or Argentina often create shots after entries. Shot rate is higher than goal rate because not every shot becomes a goal.
task_4_answer: Long passes have the highest shot rate, but this does not prove causality.
task_5_answer: Direct goals often start in midfield; longer categories also include deeper starts.
task_6_answer: Spain is patient, Mexico or Ecuador are more direct. It is a style comparison, not a quality ranking.
task_7_answer: Lines show entries to shots, green zones show total xG. The coach could train entries and support runs in dangerous zones.
main_feedback: The Spain plot is interesting but needs a direct xG explanation.
improvement_applied: Added xG explanation in the plot caption and report text.

task_1_answer: Few and medium pass sequences are more important than very long sequences.
task_2_answer: 4-6 has the highest rate; still other factors matter.
task_3_answer: Brazil creates many shots after entries. Goal rate is lower because scoring is harder than shooting.
task_4_answer: Long passes seem effective, but the chart should not be read as long passes are always better.
task_5_answer: Many direct attacks start in midfield.
task_6_answer: Spain is the most patient team in the ranking; directness is not the same as quality.
task_7_answer: The green areas mean chance quality by lane. It became clear after reading the caption.
main_feedback: The entry-method chart needs a clear warning against causal interpretation.
improvement_applied: Added descriptive-not-causal wording in the report.

task_1_answer: The 0-3 category is largest for shots and goals.
task_2_answer: 4-6 appears most efficient, but sample and context matter.
task_3_answer: France or Argentina are easy to identify. The red goal dots are smaller because goals are rarer.
task_4_answer: Long pass has the highest shot rate after entry. It is useful as a training idea, not a universal rule.
task_5_answer: The simplified zones are easier than a heatmap.
task_6_answer: Spain is patient; Mexico or Ecuador are direct. The plot shows style differences.
task_7_answer: The Spain plot shows where Spain generated xG after final-third entries.
main_feedback: Simple comparison charts were easiest. Multi-information charts need stronger captions.
